message:
fix(readwrite): GEXF multigraph detection is attribute-order / quote-style agnostic (br-2ky8p)

Fresh-eyes review of 87395f5 found the regex-based multigraph
detection in ``_gexf_document_is_multigraph`` only matches
``source="..."`` before ``target="..."`` with double quotes. XML
permits any attribute order and either quote style, and third-party
GEXF producers (Gephi, hand-authored docs) freely reorder. For
those inputs fnx misclassified the document as a simple graph and
silently dropped every parallel edge on read.

Fix: swap the regex for a streaming
``xml.etree.ElementTree.iterparse`` that picks ``source`` and
``target`` off each ``<edge>`` element regardless of order or
quote style. GEXF namespace prefixes are stripped before tag
comparison so any GEXF version is accepted. ``parallel="true"``
declaration (both quote styles) is honoured.

Regression tests cover all four attribute-order × quote-style
combinations, plus negative cases (single edge, distinct edges,
no edges), ``parallel="true"`` forcing multigraph, and malformed
XML returning False rather than raising.

Suite: 3950 passed (was 3941) / 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update the prose snapshot or replace it with wording that avoids stale copied counts while preserving docs/coverage.md as the source of truth.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:28:11.475018802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T00:34:55.097802221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T00:34:55.095787186Z","close_reason":"Updated FEATURE_PARITY public surface snapshot to match generated docs/coverage.md counts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","docs","parity"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-2iza","title":"[RW] GraphML nested data clears key","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T05:16:04.948896089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:38:25.041611818Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T05:38:25.041352077Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-2ky8p","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] _gexf_document_is_multigraph regex misses target-first or single-quoted attrs, loses parallel edges on read","description":"Fresh-eyes review of the fix landed in 87395f5 (br-rrh32) turned
up a correctness gap in the multigraph-detection peek used by
``read_gexf`` / ``parse_gexf``.

The helper ``_gexf_document_is_multigraph`` in
``python/franken_networkx/readwrite/__init__.py`` peeks the raw
bytes for repeated ``(source, target)`` pairs via a regex:

    re.findall(r'<edge\b[^>]*\bsource="([^"]+)"[^>]*\btarget="([^"]+)"', text)

This only matches when:

- ``source`` appears *before* ``target`` in the tag attribute list
- attributes are double-quoted

XML-1.0 permits any attribute order and either quote style, and
nx's own writer does emit ``source`` first with double quotes, so
the happy path works. But GEXF documents produced by Gephi,
third-party exporters, or hand-authored tools can freely reorder
attributes — e.g. ``<edge target="1" source="0"/>`` is valid GEXF.
For those inputs, fnx silently misclassifies the document as a
simple graph and drops every parallel edge on read (data loss, no
signal).

Reproducer::

    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _gexf_document_is_multigraph

    # Target-before-source ordering
    doc1 = b'<edge target="1" source="0" /><edge target="1" source="0" />'
    assert _gexf_document_is_multigraph(doc1) is True   # FAILS -> False

    # Single-quoted attributes
    doc2 = b"<edge source='0' target='1' /><edge source='0' target='1' />"
    assert _gexf_document_is_multigraph(doc2) is True   # FAILS -> False

Impact:

- Silent data loss when reading multigraph GEXF produced by
  third-party tools. Parallel edges collapse to a single edge and
  the returned graph is a simple Graph/DiGraph.
- Happy-path nx-authored GEXF documents are unaffected (nx always
  emits ``source`` first, double-quoted).

Fix plan:

1. Replace the regex with two (or four) separate regex passes: one
   for source-first, one for target-first, each in both quote
   styles. Union the captured pairs.
2. Or: swap the regex for a proper lightweight XML pull-parse
   (``xml.etree.ElementTree.iterparse``) that extracts
   ``(source, target)`` attributes regardless of ordering / quoting.
   Slightly more expensive but always correct.
3. Regression tests in
   ``tests/python/test_round_trip_identity.py`` — assert
   ``_gexf_document_is_multigraph`` returns True for all four
   attribute-order × quote-style combinations on a repeated-edge
   document.

Tag: [MEDIUM] — silent but bounded data-loss; only triggers on

Upstream supports length-bounded cycle enumeration and returns only cycles whose length is within the requested bound. Match the keyword surface and bounded-result contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:44:20.788460739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:53:40.547843864Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:53:40.545986148Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add a native Rust Pajek parser boundary (or another instrumentable Rust ingestion layer) so cargo-fuzz can cover Pajek inputs instead of leaving them outside the parser fuzz suite.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-15T04:31:38.164584457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T20:09:43.501616506Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T20:09:43.498739325Z","close_reason":"Added native Rust Pajek parser (read_pajek, read_digraph_pajek) with cargo-fuzz target fuzz_pajek. Supports *Vertices, *Edges, *Arcs sections with optional weights. Added to CI smoke and nightly soak.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -1254,23 +1254,40 @@ def _generate_gexf_via_nx(G, *, encoding, prettyprint, version):
 
 def _gexf_document_is_multigraph(raw_bytes):
     """Inexpensive peek: does this GEXF document declare parallel
-    edges or contain repeated (source, target) pairs?
+    edges or contain repeated ``(source, target)`` pairs?
+
+    Pulls ``source`` / ``target`` attributes off every ``<edge>``
+    element with ``xml.etree.ElementTree.iterparse`` so attribute
+    order and quote style don't matter (franken_networkx-2ky8p). The
+    ``parallel="true"`` declaration on ``<edges>`` is also honoured.
     """
-    # GEXF spec: parallel edges are signalled by repeated source/target
-    # pairs on <edge> elements or by the optional ``parallel="true"``
-    # attribute on <edges>. A fast substring check catches both without
-    # a full XML parse.
     try:
         text = raw_bytes.decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
     except Exception:
         return False
-    if 'parallel="true"' in text:
+    if 'parallel="true"' in text or "parallel='true'" in text:
         return True
-    # Count <edge ...> occurrences and compare against unique
-    # (source, target) pairs: cheap but accurate.
-    import re
 
-    pairs = re.findall(r'<edge\b[^>]*\bsource="([^"]+)"[^>]*\btarget="([^"]+)"', text)
+    import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
+    from io import BytesIO
+
+    pairs = []
+    try:
+        for _event, elem in ET.iterparse(BytesIO(raw_bytes), events=("end",)):
+            # Tag names in GEXF carry a namespace prefix like
+            # "{http://www.gexf.net/1.2draft}edge"; strip it before
+            # comparing so any GEXF version matches.
+            tag = elem.tag.rsplit("}", 1)[-1] if "}" in elem.tag else elem.tag
+            if tag == "edge":
+                source = elem.attrib.get("source")
+                target = elem.attrib.get("target")
+                if source is not None and target is not None:
+                    pairs.append((source, target))
+            # Free memory — we only need attribute values, not the tree.
+            elem.clear()
+    except ET.ParseError:
+        return False
+
     if not pairs:
         return False
     return len(pairs) != len(set(pairs))

@@ -420,3 +420,63 @@ def test_gexf_multigraph_round_trip(fnx_cls):
     weights = sorted(d["weight"] for _, _, _, d in edges)
     assert weights == [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]
     assert parsed.number_of_edges() == 3
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# franken_networkx-2ky8p: _gexf_document_is_multigraph must detect parallel
+# edges regardless of attribute order / quote style.
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+def _gexf_wrap(inner_edges):
+    return (
+        b'<?xml version="1.0"?><gexf xmlns="http://www.gexf.net/1.2draft">'
+        b'<graph><edges>' + inner_edges.encode("utf-8") + b'</edges></graph></gexf>'
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    "inner",
+    [
+        '<edge source="0" target="1"/><edge source="0" target="1"/>',
+        '<edge target="1" source="0"/><edge target="1" source="0"/>',
+        "<edge source='0' target='1'/><edge source='0' target='1'/>",
+        "<edge target='1' source='0'/><edge target='1' source='0'/>",
+    ],
+)
+def test_gexf_multigraph_detection_attribute_order_agnostic(inner):
+    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _gexf_document_is_multigraph
+
+    assert _gexf_document_is_multigraph(_gexf_wrap(inner)) is True
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    "inner",
+    [
+        '<edge source="0" target="1"/>',
+        '<edge source="0" target="1"/><edge source="1" target="2"/>',
+        '',
+    ],
+)
+def test_gexf_multigraph_detection_negative_cases(inner):
+    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _gexf_document_is_multigraph
+
+    assert _gexf_document_is_multigraph(_gexf_wrap(inner)) is False
+
+
+def test_gexf_multigraph_detection_parallel_true_attribute():
+    """The `parallel="true"` declaration on <edges> forces multigraph
+    interpretation even with a single edge.
+    """
+    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _gexf_document_is_multigraph
+
+    doc = _gexf_wrap('<edge source="0" target="1"/>').replace(
+        b"<edges>", b'<edges parallel="true">'
+    )
+    assert _gexf_document_is_multigraph(doc) is True
+
+
+def test_gexf_multigraph_detection_malformed_xml_returns_false():
+    from franken_networkx.readwrite import _gexf_document_is_multigraph
+
+    assert _gexf_document_is_multigraph(b"<not-valid-xml>") is False
